arms_mandatory_baselines: kge-owl2vec-style-lens (comparison lens only, never a design reference — directives §1)

dependent_vars.definition: F4 primary endpoint under each codebook arm
dependent_vars.name: item_correct

dependent_vars.definition: task + kernel-covered-slice accuracy (full metric vector V, F0 accounting)
dependent_vars.name: accuracy

dependent_vars.definition: total-system peak ledger per F0 §3.4 (V component)
dependent_vars.name: memory

dependent_vars.definition: FLOPs/query, p50/p95 latency, usd/query per F0 §3.3 — verifier NN-cleanup |lexicon|·D MACs on the ledger (V component)
dependent_vars.name: inference_compute

endpoints.test: true-kernel vs each null arm on the F4 primary endpoint, paired permutation over seeds, α=0.05; parity claims require TOST at d=0.5

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HS1 (verbatim): Measured range: 135M–360M, 2 families. Envelope: Content-vs-format is an interface property; verdict quoted for ≤1.7B, expected weakly scale-dependent; re-check rides free on any F7 slice. Anchor: E9-defl (R1) + Wieting–Kiela: random-structure nulls behave consistently across scales in the sentence-embedding literature.
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §4 HS1 kill criterion (verbatim): 'If arm (b) [Numberbatch] matches true-kernel on the F4 primary endpoint, the "designed deterministic content" performance claim is dead — surviving value narrows to determinism/versioning/verifiability (governance), and every later doc must say so. If arm (a) [random-atom codebook] matches, X-series structure is doing the work and NSM pinning is decoration.'

corpus_hashes._recipe: sha256 over the sorted concatenation of '<relpath>:<file-sha256>\n' lines for every file under data/<corpus>/
corpus_hashes.codebook-arm-packs: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:d-cb (random-atom encoder fork under its own ALGORITHM_VERSION label + own goldens; Numberbatch; KGE)

runner_constraints.note: post-F2 infrastructure decision deferred by the maintainer at GNG-0; these pins are set by ops amendment or at this record's own freeze

title: G1 — NSM-semantic pinning vs random-atom codebook / Numberbatch / KGE lens (HS1; rides on F4; 2 model families mandatory)
